input.structure: Verse 1 → Chorus → Verse 2 → Chorus → Bridge → Chorus → Outro

output:
[Verse 1]
Scrolling through your texts, I left a couple on read  
You hit me from the west side while I’m still in my head  
Mercedes on King Street, that’s the ghost of us parked  
Reflections in the city glass, but I’m dodging the scars  
Your perfume in my sweater, it’s a battle I lost  
You called me after midnight, the weather matched my thoughts  
That night at the Ritz, we spilled secrets on rugs  
Now it’s Uber rides solo, just me and these shrugs  
All these bottles, all these models, nothing fills in the gap  
Toronto in November, yeah, it always comes back  

[Chorus]
It's too late for apologies, but you linger in everything  

[Verse 2]
My boys tell me “let it go,” but I’m stuck on the screens  
Reposting old memories just to edit the scenes  
Your lipstick on my glass, it’s a crime in plain view  
I traded all my silence for the hope you’d come through  
That dinner at Sotto, you were staring at your phone  
I was counting all the moments I should’ve left you alone  
But pride got expensive, and I’m rich in regret  
You got new stories, I got your ghost in my bed  
They say fame makes it easy—I just text and erase  
Flights out to L.A., just to run from your face  

[Chorus]
It's too late for apologies, but you linger in everything  

[Bridge]
Maybe I flex too much, maybe love got taxed  
Too many DMs unread, too few words I took back  
If I’m honest with myself, you the only one I miss  
Tried to drown you in the club lights, but you rise through the mist  
Mansions feel hollow, the echoes sound like your laugh  
Drizzy learned the hard way, some things you can't buy back  

[Chorus]
It's too late for apologies, but you linger in everything  

[Outro]
Caught in this city, but I’m missing your voice  
Regret on the skyline—yeah, it’s always my choice

input.structure: Verse 1 → Verse 2 → Chorus → Verse 3 → Chorus → Outro

output:
[Verse 1]  
Cops cruise by in armor, little league kids duckin’ chalk lines  
Mama count pennies, rent past due, pride tucked in a sock shrine  
Corner store neon flicker—welfare prayers in the window  
Pastor say sin’s a riddle, but the liquor’s still cold and simple  
Old heads tell me history, but they whisper it, scars out loud  
Broken glass in the alley—sharp truths, nobody picking 'em out  
My uncle lost his job to a drone, now he’s scrolling on his phone  
Wonders if revolution is code for “leave me alone”  
They sell hope in plastic bags, label it “change,” tax it double  
But wisdom rides a bus—late, overworked, and always humble  

[Verse 2]  
Single mother, four shifts, dream stitched inside a lunchbox  
She pray for freedom, but the landlord steady picking deadbolts  
Three punches on the clock, one for the system, the rest for the plot  
Schoolyard flies a flag, but the anthem don’t match what we got  
Neighborhood watch look strange, patrol like we strangers  
Zip ties for the boys, a sermon for the danger  
Politicians talk in riddles, sellin’ smoke for a signature  
City council paint the ghetto, call the color “familiar”  
Words ricochet in chambers, echo lost in the smog  
But I swear I seen a revolution in the eyes of my dog  

[Chorus]  
Hands up for tomorrow, but my shadow block the sunrise  
They tell me “change gon’ come,” but it circle back in disguise  
We wear our struggle like garments, hope stitched in the seams  
Still, I chase peace down backstreets built from broken dreams  

[Verse 3]  
Concrete lullabies, babies rock to the siren’s tune  
Food stamps for dinner, sweet tea in a paper moon  
Prophets in the trap, sermons tagged under bridges  
Auntie light sage for spirits, but the city still fidgets  
Election season fever—lawn signs, empty gestures  
Potholes swallow promises, but never touch investors  
My cousin left the block, came back with a new tongue  
Said, “They buyin’ trauma in bulk, so I sold ‘em where I’m from”  
Laws written in cursive, loopholes straight as a pipeline  
They teach us the hustle, but never show us the lifeline  

[Chorus]  
Hands up for tomorrow, but my shadow block the sunrise  
They tell me “change gon’ come,” but it circle back in disguise  
We wear our struggle like garments, hope stitched in the seams  
Still, I chase peace down backstreets built from broken dreams  

[Outro]  
Sirens fade into lullabies, city hums in the rain  
If you searching for justice, count the cracks in the lane

input.structure: Verse 1 → Chorus → Verse 2 → Chorus → Bridge → Chorus → Outro

output:
[Verse 1]  
Talking to myself, mouth full of sunflower seeds  
Messy hair, your sweater on backwards, nobody sees  
You say you love the way I question everything you mean  
But I read your silence in the corners, tuck it under my sleeve  
Who taught me to swallow my feelings like vitamins, bitter and sweet?  
Call your name in my head and it echoes too low  
Why you only brave with your phone in the dark?  
I’m counting your maybes like petals—am I letting me go?  
I wear all my doubt like a fragrance, but I still hope you’ll know  

[Chorus]  
Wish I could trust what you say, but it moves in waves  
Every time you reach, I flinch, I fade  
You hold me near, I disappear—do you even notice me change?  

[Verse 2]  
You leave little clues in the sink, cups you never rinse  
Spill your secrets on my couch, then vanish when I catch a hint  
Love songs in your playlist, but I’m never in the verse  
Do you miss me, or the comfort? Sometimes I can’t tell which is worse  
Scroll past my texts, blue bubbles stacking like bills  
Who am I tryna be so you’ll stay—or do I just stay still?  
That movie you love, I memorize lines, try to play your part  
But your eyes go glassy, and I feel myself drifting apart  
Maybe it’s me bending too much for someone who’s scared of the start  

[Chorus]  
Wish I could trust what you say, but it moves in waves  
Every time you reach, I flinch, I fade  
You hold me near, I disappear—do you even notice me change?  

[Bridge]  
Maybe I talk too much, maybe I don’t say the right things  
Maybe your love is a mirror and I can’t face what it brings  
Do I sabotage soft mornings just to feel in control?  
Or is it you who leaves the door open, waiting for me to fold?  
If I wore my heart outside, would you just walk right by?  
Or would you finally see all the things I’m too tired to hide?  

[Chorus]  
Wish I could trust what you say, but it moves in waves  
Every time you reach, I flinch, I fade  
You hold me near, I disappear—do you even notice me change?  

[Outro]  
Whisper my doubts in the shower, rinse and repeat  
If I vanish tonight, would you ever know it was me?

input.structure: Intro → Verse 1 → Pre-Chorus → Chorus → Verse 2 → Pre-Chorus → Chorus → Bridge → Chorus → Outro

output:
[Intro]  
Headlights bleed down empty avenues  
Ashtray full, city pulse still humming  
Liquor stains on my white reflection  
Midnight’s hungry, and I’m the only one left

[Verse 1]  
Numb on my velvet couch, a half-dream flickers  
Phone screen lit, your name swimming in static  
Shadows crawl slow, painting secrets on the ceiling  
Breathing in perfume ghosts from last November  
You said I wore my devils like cologne—  
Now the silence wears Chanel  
Stacked bottles on the nightstand, gold and bitter  
Echoes of your laughter curling up with the smoke  
Trading sleep for memories that only get louder  
Heart in the backseat, begging to forget

[Pre-Chorus]  
Every mirror's got your fingerprints  
City’s full but I feel transparent

[Chorus]  
Late night, I’m talking to the dark again  
Lost in rooms you never walked in  
If you hear me, don’t answer  
Just let me drown in the after

[Verse 2]  
Designer pills melt slow on my tongue  
TV snow, but I’m watching your silhouette  
Heavy are the wrists when no one’s waiting up  
Playing voicemails just to hear you lie sweet  
Curtains drawn, the world don’t want to see this  
Red eyes steady, matching brake lights outside  
You left your lipstick on my cigarette  
And now I taste regret, velvet-smooth and cold  
Can’t escape the echo of your footsteps  
Floorboards moan, but you’re nowhere at all

[Pre-Chorus]  
Every mirror's got your fingerprints  
City’s full but I feel transparent

[Chorus]  
Late night, I’m talking to the dark again  
Lost in rooms you never walked in  
If you hear me, don’t answer  
Just let me drown in the after

[Bridge]  
I let the night bleed into my veins,  
Counting confessions in the cracks of my crown  
I call your name in a borrowed falsetto,  
Sounding like someone I don’t recognize  
Lonely’s expensive in this suite of mistakes  
Truth’s in the minibar, locked and untouched  
Moonlight whispers promises I can’t afford  
But dawn never leaves a tip

[Chorus]  
Late night, I’m talking to the dark again  
Lost in rooms you never walked in  
If you hear me, don’t answer  
Just let me drown in the after

[Outro]  
Glass on the counter, your shadow on my chest  
Streetlamps flicker out, but I’m still awake

input.structure: Verse 1 → Chorus → Verse 2 → Chorus → Bridge → Chorus → Outro

output:
[Verse 1]  
Summer of seventeen, windows down, highway one  
Your Converse on my dashboard, laughter chasing the sun  
We stole peaches from the roadside stand, juice on your hands  
You drove too fast, radio up, that old Springsteen jam  
Your mom’s perfume clinging to your faded gray hoodie  
You said, “Let’s never change,” as the salt kissed our skin  
Pulled over at the overlook, secrets written on foggy glass  
Lightning bugs in the glovebox, wishing the night would always last  

[Chorus]  
But memories live in Polaroid corners  
Folded in letters I never did send  
A song that gets softer every September  
I loved you in moments that won’t come again  

[Verse 2]  
Fall crept through the cracks—sweaters and coffee stains  
Your laugh echoing down empty bleachers after the game  
We mapped out the stars with frost on our breath  
Tracing the lines of “forever” with trembling hands  
Blue ink on your wrist, promises nobody could read  
You left your old scarf in the backseat, it still smells like rain  
I’d call just to hear your voice—nothing, then everything  
But autumn always steals what you think is yours to keep  

[Chorus]  
But memories live in Polaroid corners  
Folded in letters I never did send  
A song that gets softer every September  
I loved you in moments that won’t come again  

[Bridge]  
Now the headlines change, and so do we  
You cross my mind on ordinary streets  
Every echo, every faded refrain  
Is a ticket back to us in the rearview—just out of reach  

[Chorus]  
But memories live in Polaroid corners  
Folded in letters I never did send  
A song that gets softer every September  
I loved you in moments that won’t come again  

[Outro]  
All that’s left is dust on the dashboard and your ghost in the wind  
I drive by our old summer, just to feel everything again

input.structure: Verse 1 → Chorus → Verse 2 → Chorus → Bridge → Chorus → Outro

output:
[Verse 1]  
You screen my texts, then double tap my story—petty moves,  
Typing bubbles on a Wednesday, then you ghost, what’s new?  
I send a late night Uber, you reply with attitude,  
Screenshots to your girls, they all rooting for my lose,  
West End silence, you at Soho House with some dude,  
I left a voice note, half-drunk—erased it, thought of you,  
Why’s every message feel like a dare or a bruise?  
You say “Seen,” then I see you out, table for two.  
I’m tryna scroll past you, but you always come through,  
You’re my bad signal, but I keep calling, too.

[Chorus]  
Why we only talking when the tone turn blue?

[Verse 2]  
You live to leave me on read, but you hate when I wait,  
Got a burner account just to check if you straight,  
Mixing Sade with your playlists, you know my taste,  
Laughed at my typo, you say autocorrect’s fate,  
My phone like a stage, every word gotta play—  
Apple Pay for your coffee, that’s my modern bouquet,  
I got receipts I never send, kept for a rainy day,  
You drop a meme with a side eye, I know what you say,  
We both in our feelings, but emojis in the way,  
Still scrolling down your thread, no one else in my way.

[Chorus]  
Why we only talking when the tone turn blue?

[Bridge]  
You say “let’s be chill,” but your WiFi always strong for me,  
Signal always shaky when you want to let me be,  
Left on delivered, but I’m stuck on your read,  
Delete the thread, but we live in repeat,  
I know your lock screen better than your momma do,  
Can’t keep it clean, but I can’t mute you,  
Burned through my data just to argue with you,  
Should’ve blocked you, but I like the déjà vu.

[Chorus]  
Why we only talking when the tone turn blue?

[Outro]  
All our love in the cloud, never comes through,  
Won’t say goodbye, just leave me on mute.

input.structure: Verse 1 → Verse 2 → Chorus → Verse 3 → Chorus → Outro

output:
[Verse 1]  
Liquor stains on the contract, fingerprints lingerin’,  
Mama ironin’ my shirt while I’m iron-willed, simmerin’—  
See the city stretch its arms ‘round my calendar,  
Still the bills stack like sins, tryna balance her  
Voice in the back: “Don’t let the gold choke you,”  
Basement ceiling memories, roaches in the Folgers too  
Success feel like a debt owed to a ghost,  
Every handshake got a clause, every toast got a cost—  
Hunger don't die, it just wearin' a new coat,  
I’m prayin’ over plates, but my mind won’t digest the note.

[Verse 2]  
Early morning text sayin’, “Bro, you next, don’t slip,”  
But the view up here’s lonely, and the ledge don’t grip  
I seen brothers turn statues—silent, motionless,  
Ain’t no applause in the silence, just cold loneliness  
Fame got a shadow, keep steppin’ on my heels,  
Every chain I purchase, add another set of steel  
Labels talk in riddles, they mintin' my pain for bills,  
Missed birthdays, missed calls, missed how freedom feels  
I raise my glass, but I barely taste the thrill,  
Love me for the art or the check—what’s real?

[Chorus]  
Weight on my neck, but the crown don’t fit  
Lost in the glare, can’t see who I’m with  
Success just a burden in a silk-wrapped myth  
If I climb any higher, do I still exist?

[Verse 3]  
Used to be prayers missin’ dinner, now it’s fasting for peace,  
Auntie say, “Keep your soul, nephew, don’t auction the lease”  
I see mirrors in the crowd, they reflecting my flaws,  
Cameras flashing, but the shutter just closing the doors  
Brother called from the south, said the city miss my laugh,  
But the laughter feel hollow, echoin’ from the past  
Trophies on the mantle, dust thick as regret,  
Every win come with wounds I ain't bandaged yet  
Coach told me, “Stay humble”—I grew roots on the bench,  
Now I’m starter for the league, but the game feel tense.

[Chorus]  
Weight on my neck, but the crown don’t fit  
Lost in the glare, can’t see who I’m with  
Success just a burden in a silk-wrapped myth  
If I climb any higher, do I still exist?

[Outro]  
Check my pulse in the mirror—who cashing my breath?  
If I’m rich in the world, why I feel in debt?

input.structure: Verse 1 → Hook → Verse 2 → Hook → Hook → Outro

output:
[Verse 1]  
Silver screens in my rearview, city dusk in my veins  
Phantom doors, suicide open, letting all the secrets hang  
Chandeliers in the lobby, but the ceiling’s out the frame  
She got smoke in her lashes, whisper Cartier my name  
Starboy in a maze, latex gloves on the glass  
Left my heart at the Mondrian, lost it thick in the flash  
Shadows drift on her shoulders, neon bleeding on her skin  
We chase the static in silence, every virtue feels like sin  
Pour another for the echo, double vision for the win  

[Hook]  
All these lights, too loud for confession  
Room full of ghosts, but I dance with obsession  
Diamond haze, bodies blurred in reflection  
Night’s a film, and I’m lost in projection  

[Verse 2]  
Marble floors, but I’m gliding, never touch ‘em with my soul  
She draw blood on a mirror, thousand-dollar for control  
Plastic roses in the basin, hollow gold in my grin  
I got rooms without numbers, I forget where I begin  
The Weeknd on repeat, twilight taste in her hair  
Cinematic excess, but I fade when you stare  
Gucci ghosts in the closet, velvet gods in my dreams  
Wake up sweating ambition, but I drown in the cream  
Credit karma on my conscience, but tonight, let it bleed  

[Hook]  
All these lights, too loud for confession  
Room full of ghosts, but I dance with obsession  
Diamond haze, bodies blurred in reflection  
Night’s a film, and I’m lost in projection  

[Hook]  
All these lights, too loud for confession  
Room full of ghosts, but I dance with obsession  
Diamond haze, bodies blurred in reflection  
Night’s a film, and I’m lost in projection  

[Outro]  
Roll the credits in silence, velvet curtain falls slow  
Behind platinum curtains, my shadows still glow

input.structure: Verse 1 → Chorus → Verse 2 → Chorus → Bridge → Chorus → Outro

output:
[Verse 1]  
Bedroom with the navy walls, scratched initials on the closet door  
My sweater sleeves keep swallowing my hands, I’m thirteen and bored  
There’s a voicemail from my future self, she’s humming in my ear  
Mom’s perfume on the staircase, but I barely ever see her here  
The city buzzes past the blinds in pixel-colored light  
I paint freckles on my arms to turn the bruises into polka dots tonight  
Wish I could staple down the seconds, but the clock’s a hungry thief  
Grew too tall for all my secrets, lost the map beneath my sheets  

[Chorus]  
I blink, and the years spill out like marbles on the kitchen floor  
I whisper to the shadow in the mirror, “please—just once—slow down”  
My voice is softer now, but my doubts are loud  
Somewhere between a lullaby and a siren sound  

[Verse 2]  
Snapchat ghosts and faded friends, I scroll until my thumb goes numb  
Laughter trapped in voice memos, can’t believe how fast we come undone  
Dad’s car keys on the counter, another grown-up word I shouldn’t hear  
I mouth along to midnight songs, syncing heartbeats with my fear  
Remember June: I dyed my hair just to watch it fade away  
Playing dress-up in disaster, only real when I replay  
Every rush to be older leaves me running out of room  
Trading sleep for stories that unravel in my empty afternoon  

[Chorus]  
I blink, and the years spill out like marbles on the kitchen floor  
I whisper to the shadow in the mirror, “please—just once—slow down”  
My voice is softer now, but my doubts are loud  
Somewhere between a lullaby and a siren sound  

[Bridge]  
I sneak downstairs at 3 AM, just to prove the world’s still spinning  
Streetlights draw lines on my ceiling—maybe growing up just means thinning  
Invisible in my own sweatshirt, counting freckles, counting days  
If I could fold myself smaller, would the memories ever stay?  

[Chorus]  
I blink, and the years spill out like marbles on the kitchen floor  
I whisper to the shadow in the mirror, “please—just once—slow down”  
My voice is softer now, but my doubts are loud  
Somewhere between a lullaby and a siren sound  

[Outro]  
In the hush before the sunrise, I’m just a secret in my skin  
Growing up too fast, or maybe just growing in

[Verse 1]  
Luces bajitas, la disco encendía,  
Llegué con flow pa' derretir la fría,  
Botellas arriba, el VIP en mi esquina,  
Tu jevo mirando, pero tú eres mía.  
Tatuajes brillan con el sudor del party,  
Tú bailas lento, nadie aquí te compara,  
Gafas oscuras aunque ya no es de día,  
Aquí mandamos, los haters no tienen guía.  
Hablan de mí, pero nunca me alcanzan,  
Con paso seguro, la envidia se cansa.

[Chorus]  
No hay competencia cuando piso el suelo,  
Tú y yo brillando más que los destellos,  
Aquí se nota quién tiene el juego,  
Baby, lo nuestro nunca fue un misterio.

[Verse 2]  
Hasta el DJ pide foto y no lo niego,  
Corro en la pista como si fuera juego,  
Cadenas de oro, sonrisa de fuego,  
Tú eres mi musa, yo soy tu despecho.  
Cero inseguro, confío en mi reflejo,  
Los falsos saludan pa’ buscar consejo,  
Pero no hay truco, esto es puro talento,  
La noche es joven, y yo con mi acento.  
Te acercas más, la vibra no miente,  
Si miran feo, que sigan de frente.

[Chorus]  
No hay competencia cuando piso el suelo,  
Tú y yo brillando más que los destellos,  
Aquí se nota quién tiene el juego,  
Baby, lo nuestro nunca fue un misterio.

input.structure: Verse 1 → Chorus → Verse 2 → Chorus → Bridge → Chorus → Outro

output:
[Verse 1]  
Pink print on my wrist, competition get dismissed  
I’m the headline, you the pre-show, baby, peep the guest list  
Mirror-mirror, who the illest? Stiletto on your throat  
Chanel on my ambition, every bar is a note  
They throwin’ little jabs, but I counter with a pose  
Pull up, face beat, attitude high, heaven knows  
I’m the villain in your story, but I rock the hero’s cape  
Quick switch, venom in the honey—watch them double-take  
Stack the accolades, then ice out the safe  
Queen move, checkmate, crowd scream, “That’s my fave!”

[Chorus]  
I’m the reason they rehearse, I’m the standard, it’s a curse  
Step up in the ring, they get nervous, do they worst  
They can talk, but I been flame — let ‘em burst  
Number one and I ain’t ever coming last, universe

[Verse 2]  
Signature on the line, red ink for my rivals  
I don’t chase, I replace, turn arrivals to survival  
Neon in my bloodline, every flex make 'em twitch  
I collect gold teeth, leave ‘em biting on the switch  
They copy the persona but the blueprint missing pages  
I been flipping scripts, while they stuck in minor stages  
Every tweet, every shade, only add to my parade  
You sellin’ for a mention, I just renegotiate  
I’m the architect, honey, you just lay the bricks  
Diamonds in my energy, you still playing pick-up sticks

[Chorus]  
I’m the reason they rehearse, I’m the standard, it’s a curse  
Step up in the ring, they get nervous, do they worst  
They can talk, but I been flame — let ‘em burst  
Number one and I ain’t ever coming last, universe

[Bridge]  
Baddest in the room, crown heavy, never slips  
They beg for an alliance just to get a little drip  
I’m the plot twist, label exec, and the myth  
Laughing to the bank, pink smoke in the lift  
Trophies on the dresser, every hater gets a mention  
But they never in my section, all they efforts get correction

[Chorus]  
I’m the reason they rehearse, I’m the standard, it’s a curse  
Step up in the ring, they get nervous, do they worst  
They can talk, but I been flame — let ‘em burst  
Number one and I ain’t ever coming last, universe

[Outro]  
Curtain call, lipstick print on the throne  
Crowd chanting “Nicki” as I ride home alone

[Verse 1]  
Plastic lawn chair, sweating out July,  
Cicadas buzz like static in my half-dream,  
You left your lighter in my glovebox, orange,  
I guess that means you trusted me for a minute—  
My neighbor’s porch light blinks Morse code gossip,  
Nails bitten, tongue pressed to a chipped incisor,  
You said your memory worked like a scratched record,  
Skipping the summers, sinking in bathtubs,  
I tried to trace your name in condensation,  
But the window fogs over before I finish.

[Chorus]  
Fading in, fading out—your face in the living room flicker  
Can’t hold what drifts through, can’t rewind,  
I keep losing the color every time I remember.

[Verse 2]  
Old cable box counting seconds in blue digits,  
My uncle’s shadow arguing with the wall—  
Salt from french fries, sweat from your wrist,  
You said, “Remind me why we keep old birthday cards?”  
A shoe untied, radio static,  
Your laugh echoing from another apartment—  
I forget the punchline, remember how you looked away,  
Hiding in plain sight, the night hums through the drywall,  
We borrow time from a calendar with pages torn out,  
Every soft erasure leaves a fingerprint.

[Chorus]  
Fading in, fading out—your face in the living room flicker  
Can’t hold what drifts through, can’t rewind,  
I keep losing the color every time I remember.

input.structure: Verse 1 → Chorus → Verse 2 → Chorus → Bridge → Chorus → Outro

output:
[Verse 1]
There’s a lonesome stretch of two-lane out past Miller’s farm,  
Where the sun sets slow behind a line of leaning barns,  
Same dirt under fingernails, same prayers before we eat,  
We carve our names in oak trees ‘stead of city concrete.  
Mama’s porch light’s flickering, dogs bark down the road,  
Old John Deere humming, hauling dreams I never sold.  
The diner’s still serving coffee thick as midnight mud,  
Folks talking high school glory, stories stick like blood.

[Chorus]
We don’t need neon, just a sky full of stars,  
Raised on handshakes, sweat, and old Fords parked in yards,  
Every mile from here to anywhere else,  
Still got that small town pride running through myself.

[Verse 2]
Cousin’s got a tackle box, granddad’s got a flask,  
Everybody’s got a story, everybody’s got a past.  
We fish the muddy river, hit the county fair each June,  
Tailgates down with “Freebird” singing out beneath the moon.  
Ain’t in no hurry, we breathe slow round these parts,  
Who needs city lights when you’ve got lightning bugs and heart?  
The preacher’s voice carries ‘cross the old wood pews,  
Sundays in our best jeans, counting blessings, counting truths.

[Chorus]
We don’t need neon, just a sky full of stars,  
Raised on handshakes, sweat, and old Fords parked in yards,  
Every mile from here to anywhere else,  
Still got that small town pride running through myself.

[Bridge]
Maybe I’ll wander, maybe I’ll roam,  
But the sound of that red clay calling pulls me back home.  
Dust on my boots, that’s just part of my name,  
You can dress up the boy, but the roots, they remain.  
All these winding roads, they always know me best—  
Home’s not just a word, it’s the gravel in my chest.

[Chorus]
We don’t need neon, just a sky full of stars,  
Raised on handshakes, sweat, and old Fords parked in yards,  
Every mile from here to anywhere else,  
Still got that small town pride running through myself.

[Outro]
So here’s to front porch nights and county lines,  
Heart still beating to this hometown’s rhymes.

input.structure: Verse 1 → Chorus → Verse 2 → Chorus → Bridge → Chorus → Outro

output:
[Verse 1]  
Four texts from my mother, she sayin’ I’m distant  
Toronto nights cold, but the wrist got a glisten  
Uber black, riding solo, see my name in the system  
Turned friends to advisors, now the vibe’s inconsistent  
Champagne stains on the tabletop, legacy talk  
No trophy on the shelf, just these flights that I chalk  
Screenshots in the group chat, who real, who just watch  
In the mirror, got questions that I’m scared to unlock  
Twenty missed calls, but I’m proud and I’m stubborn  
Life’s movin’ fast—Drizzy never been one for runnin’  

[Chorus]  
Life just movin’ different, yeah,  
Too many views from the distance, yeah  

[Verse 2]  
Second bottle down, I’m replying to old flames  
Told the city I’d make it, now I’m dodgin’ these claims  
Late talks with my pops, sayin’ “son, keep your aim”  
But the target keep movin’—that’s the cost of the game  
Spotify numbers lookin’ healthy, but I’m empty  
Diamonds in the bezel, but the mind feelin’ heavy  
Booked flights to Dubai, but the mood never left me  
Still see my day ones, but the convo’s less friendly  
Selfies with strangers, all the love feelin’ rented  
At the top floor suite, wonderin’ when I’ll be contented  

[Chorus]  
Life just movin’ different, yeah,  
Too many views from the distance, yeah  

[Bridge]  
Used to dream of these heights, now the roof feelin’ closed  
Can’t tell if I’m blessed or just stuck in my goals  
Every airport lounge, man, the silence expose  
That the moments I cherish are the ones no one knows  
Reminiscin’ on summer, when the squad was together  
Now we FaceTime our feelings, never say it in letters  
Tryna buy back my time, but it’s never for sale  
Every lesson expensive, every memory frail  

[Chorus]  
Life just movin’ different, yeah,  
Too many views from the distance, yeah  

[Outro]  
Left unread in my own life, scrollin’ through seasons  
If you find me, let it be for the right reasons

[Verse 1]  
Sleep in my sweater, it’s colder at noon  
Quiet confessions dissolve in my room  
You said I look happiest stuck on the ceiling  
I’m weightless and heavy, my head underwater  
Bruises from laughing, I tense when it’s over  
Heart skips a season, you text me, "come over"  
I float in the static, the blue is electric  
Breathing too loud, so I hush out the panic  

[Chorus]  
You taste like fireworks after the rain  
Bittersweet thunder, soft on my veins  
I shiver in color, but call it a phase  
Euphoric sadness—I’m caught in the haze  

[Verse 2]  
Soap on my tongue, and your ghost in my mirror  
I dance with my shadow, you’re close but unclear  
Tracing the patterns you left in my bedsheets  
I wish you were simple, I wish I was brave  
We laugh in the silence, we drown in the echo  
Your eyes flicker gold when you say you could let go  
My hands full of secrets, my voice barely whisper  
Dizzy and bright, but I’m sinking much quicker  

[Chorus]  
You taste like fireworks after the rain  
Bittersweet thunder, soft on my veins  
I shiver in color, but call it a phase  
Euphoric sadness—I’m caught in the haze

input.structure: Verse 1 → Chorus → Verse 2 → Chorus → Bridge → Chorus → Outro
input.extra: Use complex internal rhymes and double entendres.

output:
[Verse 1]  
Concrete veins, heartbeat in the gridlock,  
Sirens serenade the faithless at dawn—tick-tock.  
Broken glass wisdom, every step a parable,  
Graffiti prophets spray sermons, indelible.  
Mama said “march soft,” but the block got loud,  
Prayers ricochet off steel, still hungry and proud.  
Red and blue flash, silhouette on my shadow,  
History written in chalk, lips zipped—afraid to tattle.  
I barter my smile for peace, but inflation high,  
Devil deals in policies—can’t blink an eye.  

[Chorus]  
See the fire in the eyes, smoke in the lungs,  
Truth gets strangled by a silver tongue.  
Chain-linked freedom, they barter and trade,  
Justice on layaway, debt never paid.  

[Verse 2]  
Elevation in the alleys, we baptize in rain,  
Cop cars circle sermons, baptize in pain.  
Elders got stories, coded in Morse—  
Every dash, every dot, genocide by force.  
My cousin’s skin—currency in a crooked game,  
District lines, zip-tied dreams, shackled by name.  
Reparations written with invisible ink,  
Courthouses grin while my future shrink.  
Twelve on the payroll, truth on suspension,  
Hope in detention, bars bred from intention.  

[Chorus]  
See the fire in the eyes, smoke in the lungs,  
Truth gets strangled by a silver tongue.  
Chain-linked freedom, they barter and trade,  
Justice on layaway, debt never paid.  

[Bridge]  
We the pulse in the panic, the ghost in the gavel,  
Half-mast flags, half-truths unravel.  
March in the silence, drum on the sky,  
Inheritance of trauma—don’t ask why.  
Blueprints of revolution inscribed in the marrow,  
We the children of the arrow who refuse the straight and narrow.  
I see freedom dance on the tip of a match,  
Cold sweat on the badge, waiting for the catch.  

[Chorus]  
See the fire in the eyes, smoke in the lungs,  
Truth gets strangled by a silver tongue.  
Chain-linked freedom, they barter and trade,  
Justice on layaway, debt never paid.  

[Outro]  
When sunrise feels foreign, we paint it familiar,  
History repeats, but the voice gets peculiar.
